title: potentilla inclinata subsp. adscendens
intro: Potentilla inclinata subsp. adscendens is a subspecies of the Potentilla inclinata plant, also known as the cinquefoil, which is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the rose family. The leaves and roots of this plant have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Potentilla inclinata subsp. adscendens include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and act as a natural remedy for skin conditions and wounds. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is also used in salves and creams to promote wound healing and skin health.
